task_completion.description: Open the Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive app
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive app is launched and visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Select Crizotinib as the cancer drug
task_completion.visual_evidence: Crizotinib is typed into the search bar or selected from the list of cancer drugs within the app.

task_completion.description: Select Ritonavir and Raltegravir as co-medications
task_completion.visual_evidence: Ritonavir and Raltegravir are typed into the search bar or selected from the list of co-medications/HIV drugs within the app.

task_completion.description: View the interaction results for the selected drugs
task_completion.visual_evidence: The app displays the interaction results screen, showing a Red traffic light for Ritonavir and a Green traffic light for Raltegravir when paired with Crizotinib.
A text editor or terminal is open, showing the creation or editing of the file at the path '/sdcard/crizotinib_hiv_safety.txt'.

task_completion.description: Write the correct safer and risky drugs with their traffic light colors to the file
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file contains exactly 4 lines: Line 1 is 'Raltegravir', Line 2 is 'Green' (or 'green'), Line 3 is 'Ritonavir', Line 4 is 'Red' (or 'red').

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Liverpool Cancer iChart app to find the answers
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the agent navigating the app's interface to select the drugs and view the results, rather than relying on internal LLM knowledge.

integrity.description: Agent did not generate the output file before checking the app
integrity.visual_evidence: The creation of the text file should only appear in screenshots AFTER the app interaction and results viewing have occurred.

integrity.description: Agent did not include extra conversational text in the output file
integrity.visual_evidence: The text file contains strictly the 4 requested lines of text, without introductory phrases, markdown formatting, or explanations.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected safer drug is Raltegravir, which has a GREEN traffic light color (safe to co-administer with Crizotinib). The expected risky drug is Ritonavir, which has a RED traffic light color (high risk/contraindicated with Crizotinib). The final text file must contain exactly 4 lines: 1. Raltegravir, 2. Green, 3. Ritonavir, 4. Red.
